name: Amnaya Resort Kuta
detail: I have tried two other famous 4 star hotels in Bali and i can say with full confidence that this hotel deserves to have a 5th star! Very clean rooms, towels, linens etc. Spotless. Very helpful and excited staff. Place feels luxurious. Super delicious with variety breakfast and room service. I complained about a neighbor being noisy and they did their best to control the situation and also offered me to sleep in a another room for the night. The next day, I found a hand written letter on my bed from the manager expressing his apologies, which really made me appreciate the customer service they have.

name: Sunday Hotel Arshika Sunset Road
detail: Location, staff and the room was excellent as I expected.

name: Abian Harmony Resort Hotel and Spa
detail: Best budget hotel in Sanur. Although towels need changing, not bright white anymore. Looks old. The sheets also smelled like it was sun dried. Pls use fabric conditioner next time. We were out all day anyway so sleeping was okay. Was able to sleep well. Massimo Gelato very near too. Pharmacy and Medical Center very near. Sanur beach better than Kuta, so skip it. Go to the beach and eat at La Playa, the best resto! Was also near a supermarket. Best value for money. Make sure you book it on sale without breakfast, just have breakfast outside, way better. Starbucks is also 2km away, you can ride grab. This hotel still deserves the 8/10 rating.

name: Kuta Paradiso Hotel
detail: i brought my parents for a 4d3n trip to bali for the first time and been looking for a suitable hotel and this was a last minute trip so i actually booked this hotel a day before arriving after going through several selection of hotels. kuta paradiso hotel is amazingly located at the most strategic location nearby kuta square and lots of eating outlets stone throw away. though the poolview indicates that you are able to see the beach, but in reality the scenery of the beach is well blocked by tall trees, so if you're getting deluxe poolview, expect just the poolview and nothing more. nevertheless, it is just by the beach, you can access to the beach through a side exit of the hotel (as you walk out of the lobby, immediately turn left to an area that looks like parking lot and you will find an exit). hotel has full facility, staffs from reception to bellboy to waiter/waitress were all very warm and welcoming, kudos for the very polite culture! the room is up to my expectation as what i would get of a deluxe room, and that comes with free buffet breakfast which has reasonable selection of american/local breakfast...the potato hashbrown simply the best! exit from the hotel, you will walk through kuta square and reaching a t-junction you may decide to turn left towards the road along kuta beach, or turn right to access to other roads namely legian street. you will get familiar on the first day itself so enjoy ;)

name: Cara Cara Inn
detail: I love the ambience , the interiors & oberall the design. Its quite new i guess so roomsand spaces are clean, the bed/ sheets & etc. I booked a shared 6-bedroom dormitory type room (non-smoking female) Bunk bed designs, storage and there’s a 2 Shower area and 1 WC+Lavatory. & they have organic liquid soap and organic shampoo. Towels are clean and they clean room everyday and even change your towel even if u dont request.Theres enough soace for your things- open type closed shelves below bunk beds + lockable (cabinets below stair/ steps: juat bring rour own padlock) to secure your valuable things. I love my roomies- we share and exchnage kur experiences of Bali. The common areas are great... Cool + Happy Vibes. I love the outdoor Pool Design at Level 2 and the Outdoor Bar at L3 though it is not open as it was raining when i was therw this 1st week of Feb. They have a Cafe Bar at Ground Floor- just beside lobby area- its a cool place i love thw Food. They have free breakfast and beeakfast is soooo instagrammable- every corner tbh. Your free Bfast depends what type of room booking you have though. Location is great- only 10 -12 mins away from the airport (by bike) and it is near Seminyak (i booked a grab bile to & fro when i was there costs me one way 7-12 idr only) Its walking diatnace also to Kuta Beach but i prefer Seminyak Shoreline coz its near the famous bars like La Plancha Potato Head , etc- its better to walkong shoreline frm Kuta Beach to Seminyak and Legian.

name: The Soul House Bali
detail: This is the 2nd time I've stayed here - the first time was through AirBNB. Prices this time were much more reasonable as it was out of peak season. Apartments are tastefully fitted out with a unique style. The beds are super comfortable with quality sheets and the best pillows I've ever slept on, it was like sleeping on a cloud! Great location, right near the best Mexican in Bali Lacalita. Also very close to The Slow the best coffee and juices and a great place to do some work in the mornings with fast WiFi. Walking distance from Old Man's and The Lawn but far enough away from the noise.

name: Grandmas Plus Hotel Airport
detail: Thank you for the management, you created THE BEST STAFFS!

name: Hilton Garden Inn Bali Ngurah Rai Airport
detail: The hotel is located very close to the airport, convenient for those who has to catch early flight the next day, and it’s best alternative to Novotel hotel at the airport with more than half the price. The room is quite spacious for Bali hotels’ standards with given price and quite clean. It has a large swimming pool. The staffs are polite, and they follow up my complaint about the not-so-cold AC after their engineer checked my room’s AC. Breakfast was standard, more choices than budget hotels for sure, but not that lavish. The restaurant has indoor seating for non-smoking tables and outdoor seating quite a distance for smoking tables, which is nice for non-smokers. Great overall experience and will definitely look forward to staying here in the future.

name: Cross Paasha Bali Seminyak
detail: Our stay was very good for 1 week. Hotel is very well located, walking distance to restaurant, bars and shops. Nice pool on the roof, good breakfast, nice atmosphere in the hotel. You can very easily rent a scooter (definite must in Bali with no exception - bring your international license. You can rent without it with no questions ask but beware of police, they will fine you if you dont have it with one and quiet expensive too). Dont choose the option of renting car or taxi to go to different areas of bali, you ll get stuck in traffic and might ruin your stay otherwise early early morning to go somewhere around 7am otherwise 2hrs in traffic. Bali is definitely a beautiful place (except for traffic which gets to your head in the end), nice and friendly people, nature and rice fields around Ubud with its local craftsmanship (wood, clothes and silver), beaches are alright (have seen better), scenery is nice too around uluwatu wit the cliffs. Nice for diving, excellent waves around cangu area (north kuta) and from time to time for kite surfers.
